title: CPE103 — Course Learning Outcomes
description: The five course learning outcomes for Basic Computer Engineering. Every criterion is scored on the same 5-level scale: Exemplary (4), Mastery (3, the target), Near Mastery (2.5), Below Mastery (2), No Evidence (0). A CLO's score is the average of its criteria. Mastery on every CLO earns a B; Exemplary work lifts the grade to B+ and A.

title: CLO1 — Programming Fundamentals
description: Write basic programs using fundamental constructs including variables, control flow, functions, and algorithms.

title: CLO1.1 Correctness
description: How the program behaves against the required cases. Part of CLO1: Write basic programs using fundamental constructs including variables, control flow, functions, and algorithms.
friendly_description: Your program does what the assignment requires in every specified case. Mastery (3) is the target; Exemplary (4) is available on the Final Project — claim it with evidence.
column13: Exemplary: Meets Mastery and also handles edge cases and unexpected input gracefully. Available on the Final Project only — claimed with evidence.
column15: Mastery (target): Produces the required behavior in all specified cases.
column17: Near Mastery: Runs but fails one required case.
column19: Below Mastery: Program does not run, or fails most required cases.
column21: No Evidence: Nothing submitted to assess.

title: CLO1.2 Use of constructs
description: Choosing and using the right programming constructs in readable code. Part of CLO1: Write basic programs using fundamental constructs including variables, control flow, functions, and algorithms.
friendly_description: You choose the right constructs — variables, control flow, functions, algorithms — and use them correctly in readable code. Mastery (3) is the target; Exemplary (4) is available on the Final Project.
column13: Exemplary: Meets Mastery and also applies a new algorithm beyond those taught, correctly, and can explain how it works. Final Project only — claimed with evidence.
column15: Mastery (target): Selects and uses appropriate constructs (variables, control flow, custom blocks/functions, algorithms) correctly, in readable code.
column17: Near Mastery: Uses constructs but with errors or needless redundancy.
column19: Below Mastery: Misuses or avoids core constructs.
column21: No Evidence: Nothing submitted to assess.

title: CLO2 — System Analysis (User Journey & Process Mapping)
description: Apply User Journey Analysis and Process Mapping to a system, and recommend design improvements justified by that analysis.

title: CLO2.1 User journey analysis & improvement
description: Mapping the user's experience and recommending improvements justified by it. Part of CLO2: Apply User Journey Analysis and Process Mapping to a system, and recommend design improvements justified by that analysis.
friendly_description: You map the user's full journey (stages, touchpoints, pain points) and recommend a justified improvement, revising the design once. Mastery (3) is the target; Exemplary (4) is available on the Final Project.
column13: Exemplary: Meets Mastery and also closes the loop — re-tests the revised design and shows with before/after evidence that the targeted pain point measurably eased — or reconciles multiple user segments/journeys in one design. Final Project only — claimed with evidence.
column15: Mastery (target): Maps a complete, accurate journey of the user's experience (key stages, touchpoints, pain points) and recommends improvements justified by it, revising the design through one iteration.
column17: Near Mastery: Captures some of the journey but with gaps, and improvements are generic or weakly grounded in it.
column19: Below Mastery: Journey missing or inaccurate, and no usable recommendation.
column21: No Evidence: Nothing submitted to assess.

title: CLO2.2 Process Mapping
description: Documenting the system's internal process flow, distinct from the user's experience. Part of CLO2: Apply User Journey Analysis and Process Mapping to a system, and recommend design improvements justified by that analysis.
friendly_description: You document a clear, correct map of the system's internal flow — steps, sequence, and decision points. Mastery (3) is the target; Exemplary (4) is available on the Final Project.
column13: Exemplary: Meets Mastery and also models the flow beyond the happy path — exception/error paths, or responsibility lanes separating what each actor/component does. Final Project only — claimed with evidence.
column15: Mastery (target): Documents a clear, correct map of the system's internal process flow — its steps, sequence, and main decision points, distinct from the user's experience.
column17: Near Mastery: Partial map; flow unclear.
column19: Below Mastery: Absent or incorrect notation.
column21: No Evidence: Nothing submitted to assess.

title: CLO3 — Teamwork & Collaboration
description: Collaborate in a team to plan, manage, and deliver assignments and projects, fulfilling individual responsibilities and contributing to shared goals.

title: CLO3.1 Individual responsibility & collaboration (individual)
description: Each student's own reliability and cooperation, scored individually. Compound criterion — when the two halves perform at different levels, score to the weaker half. Part of CLO3: Collaborate in a team to plan, manage, and deliver assignments and projects.
friendly_description: You complete your own share on time (visible in commits or the task board) and communicate respectfully toward shared goals. Mastery (3) is the target; Exemplary (4) is available on the Final Project.
column13: Exemplary: Meets Mastery and also measurably improves how the team works — unblocks a teammate, fixes a recurring process problem, or synthesizes others' ideas into a shared decision. Final Project only — claimed with evidence.
column15: Mastery (target): Reliably completes own assigned share on time (seen in commits/task board), and communicates and cooperates respectfully toward shared goals. Scored to the weaker half.
column17: Near Mastery: Inconsistent; needs reminders, and communicates sporadically.
column19: Below Mastery: Little or no contribution; disengaged or misses commitments.
column21: No Evidence: No evidence to assess.

title: CLO3.2 Planning & delivery (team)
description: The team's shared planning, tracking, and delivery. At the Lab 9 planning milestone only the planning half is in scope; at the Final Project Openhouse the same criterion is scored against what was actually delivered. Part of CLO3: Collaborate in a team to plan, manage, and deliver assignments and projects.
friendly_description: Your team plans and tracks the work (e.g. a task board) to stay on schedule, and delivers the complete assignment or project. Mastery (3) is the target; Exemplary (4) is available on the Final Project.
column13: Exemplary: Meets Mastery and also builds resilience into the plan up front — checkpoints, contingencies, or workload rebalancing that the team can point to as having kept delivery on track. Final Project only — claimed with evidence.
column15: Mastery (target): Plans and tracks the work (e.g. a task board) to stay on schedule, and delivers the complete assignment or project as required. At Lab 9 only the planning half is in scope.
column17: Near Mastery: Plans loosely; tasks tracked unevenly; delivers late or incomplete.
column19: Below Mastery: No planning; work uncoordinated; fails to deliver.
column21: No Evidence: No evidence to assess.

title: CLO4 — Communication
description: Communicate engineering concepts clearly in written form and in oral presentation.

title: CLO4.1 Clarity & organization
description: Structure and clarity of the content, applied to both written deliverables and presentations. For purely written deliverables this is the only CLO4 criterion assessed. Part of CLO4: Communicate engineering concepts clearly in written form and in oral presentation.
friendly_description: Your document or talk is clear and well-organized — the audience can follow easily. Mastery (3) is the target; Exemplary (4) is available only in Lab 7, the self-selected-topic presentation.
column13: Exemplary: Meets Mastery and also connects the content to the audience to draw them in — a relatable analogy, or a hook from current events or the audience's own context. Lab 7 only — claimed with evidence.
column15: Mastery (target): Clear and well-organized; the audience can follow easily.
column17: Near Mastery: Some structure; clarity uneven.
column19: Below Mastery: Disorganized; hard to follow.
column21: No Evidence: No evidence to assess.

title: CLO4.2 Oral delivery
description: Spoken delivery and handling of questions in presentations. Omitted for purely written deliverables. Part of CLO4: Communicate engineering concepts clearly in written form and in oral presentation.
friendly_description: You deliver clearly and confidently at a good pace, and answer questions adequately. Mastery (3) is the target; Exemplary (4) is available only in Lab 7, the self-selected-topic presentation.
column13: Exemplary: Meets Mastery and also handles a challenging or unanticipated question with composure — engaging with it directly rather than deflecting, even if the answer is incomplete. Lab 7 only — claimed with evidence.
column15: Mastery (target): Clear, confident, well-paced delivery; answers questions adequately.
column19: Below Mastery: Unclear; reads slides; no engagement.
column21: No Evidence: No evidence to assess.

title: CLO5 — Capstone Integration
description: Integrate programming, system analysis, teamwork, and communication into a single coherent software application, taking it from initial concept to a working product. CLO5 scores the integration itself; the quality of the parts is scored in CLO1–CLO4, not re-graded here.

title: CLO5.1 Working integrated product (team)
description: Whether the separately-built parts work together end-to-end as one application. Scored at the team level. Part of CLO5: Integrate programming, system analysis, teamwork, and communication into a single coherent software application.
friendly_description: Your team's parts work together end-to-end as one application, meeting the project's core requirements. Mastery (3) is the target; Exemplary (4) is available on the Final Project.
column13: Exemplary: Meets Mastery and also embodies the assigned theme — localized to a relatable setting (e.g. CMU, current news, university events) and clearly expressed in play, not just named. Final Project only — claimed with evidence.
column15: Mastery (target): The parts work together end-to-end as one application, meeting the project's core requirements.
column17: Near Mastery: Parts work in isolation but don't connect; the core end-to-end flow is broken.
column21: No Evidence: Nothing submitted to assess.

title: CLO5.2 Coherence & integration (team)
description: Whether the components fit one deliberate design carried from initial concept to a working build. Scored at the team level. A fix inside one area belongs to that area's CLO; synergy here means one area reshaping another. Part of CLO5: Integrate programming, system analysis, teamwork, and communication into a single coherent software application.
friendly_description: Your team's product is coherent — its components fit one deliberate design, carried from concept to a working build. Mastery (3) is the target; Exemplary (4) is available on the Final Project.
column13: Exemplary: Meets Mastery and also shows synergy — two project areas that shaped each other, proven with a before-and-after of the change (e.g. a UX finding that reshaped the plan). Final Project only — claimed with evidence.
column15: Mastery (target): A coherent product whose components fit one deliberate design, carried from initial concept through to a working build.
column17: Near Mastery: Loosely assembled; the pieces fit a single design only partially or inconsistently.
column19: Below Mastery: Components disconnected; no evident overall design.
column21: No Evidence: Nothing submitted to assess.
